text: Hello friends. This is a case of large dermoid cyst. This is approximately twenty year old girl unmarried and she has one mole around the umbilicus that is normal.

The surgeon introduces a supraumbilical port approximately five centimeters above the umbilicus to access a large dermoid cyst on the right side. Irrigation and suction are used to clear the surgical field.
The surgeon performs the hanging drop test, where fluid is visibly sucked into the trocar, confirming proper placement.

Following insufflation with a preset pressure of fifteen, the actual pressure gradually increases from single digits. The flow rate is set at one liter per minute. After introducing two liters of gas, the surgeon inserts the trocar into the abdominal cavity.

The surgeon demonstrates the baseball diamond concept for port placement, using the index finger to identify the target area. The snuff box of the hand is used to determine the location of two ports, aligning with the dermo-axis for optimal access to the dermoid cyst.
Now of course we are not using uterine manipulator in this girl.

The surgeon introduces an Ethicon endo catch bag into the abdominal cavity through a contralateral port to minimize spillage during cystectomy. The endo bag is opened, and aspiration of the large dermoid cyst begins, collecting 5 to 10 ml of oily fluid for cytology. The aspiration needle is removed, and the incision is enlarged using a harmonic scalpel while maintaining the endo bag's position.
A 5mm suction device is introduced into the surgical field, rapidly extracting oily fluid from the cyst. The suction tubing visibly carries the fluid away, leading to the collapse of the cyst.
The surgeon observes a small amount of fluid spillage outside the surgical field and promptly uses a suction device to remove the fluid, ensuring the area remains clean.

The surgeon begins stripping the inner cystic layer from the outer cortex of the dermoid cyst, carefully separating it from the ovarian cortex. The cystic layer is visible, and the surgeon achieves a good plane for separation, noting the relatively better plane provided by the congenital nature of the dermoid cyst compared to endometrioma.

The surgeon uses ambidextrous techniques, alternating between the left and right hands to hold and strip the cystic layer from the ovarian cortex. A Maryland dissector is used in one hand, while a semi-traumatic grasper is used in the other, carefully separating the cystic layer.

The surgeon completes the stripping of the cyst from the ovarian cortex, with minimal oozing observed. The cyst is fully detached from the ovary, with the right side showing the cyst and the left side showing the remaining ovary. The cyst is placed into the endo bag, and the mouth of the endo bag is closed using a purse string.

The surgeon places the cyst into the endo bag and closes the mouth of the bag using a metal ring and purse string technique. The metal ring is cut from the endo bag, and gentle finger dilation is applied to facilitate the removal of the cyst from the surgical field.
So this is extraction in the endoval. Thank you very much for watching this video. This was just a simple case of Thermo-Acid System.
